content.claim: Parental demand for selective schools is sustained by perceived prestige, teacher quality, and preparation for further education, even as parents express concern that such schools prioritize academic excellence over student well-being and community cohesion.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Qualitative small-sample study from single town; not nationally representative
Parental perceptions are not validated against objective school quality measures
Does not measure whether perceived benefits materialize in actual student outcomes
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
